subject: Geography
title: Dying Rivers
description: A micro-lesson that makes Dying Rivers usable.

hook.text: The Aral Sea was the world's fourth-largest lake. Soviet irrigation drained it to dust.

buildup.text: Ships now rust in a desert where fishing boats once floated.

discovery.text: The Colorado, Yellow, and Indus rivers all regularly fail to reach the ocean.

twist.text: When a river dries up, the salt left behind poisons the soil for generations.

climax.text: The Aral region now has toxic dust storms that cause widespread illness.

punchline.text: Some rivers don't flood anymore. They just vanish.

quiz.question: What happened to the Aral Sea?
options: Soviet irrigation drained it nearly dry
